6	
7	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
8	- There is no way to remove a unwanted stop from waypoints.
9	
10	### What is the root cause of that problem?
11	- We don't have any UI element to remove a stop from waypoints.
12	
13	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
14	- To allow stop deletion from three dot menu from inside waypoint address enter screen:
15	    - On three-dot-menu press we will open a modal and show single option "Remove Stop".
16	    - On press of "Remove Stop" we will open a delete confirmation modal to confirm the deletion of the stop and we will call `removeWaypoint` function on confirmation.
17	
18	(Not posting much details on code as its already gonna be the same.)
19	
20	- To allow stop deletion from list of stops:
21	    - We can add a `(-)` minus circle icon instead of showing the `Expensicons.DotIndicator` icon.
22	    - We will also apply icon theme as `theme.danger`. To notify user that this is a remove icon.
23	    - Then we can make that icon pressable in `MenuItem.js` so that we can open a modal to confirm the deletion of the stop and we can call `removeWaypoint` function on confirmation.
24	
25	- Why two different approaches?
26	    - As user might have added a stop by mistake and he wants to remove it and we don't want to make user go inside the address enter screen to remove the stop. So we are providing a way to remove stop from list of stops.
27	
28	- Further Enhancements:
29	    - Also if we are calling same modal from two screens we can create a common delete stop modal file and use it in both the screens. To eliminate code duplication.
30	
31	    - Further I was thinking of making a common modal where we can pass title, description and button names so that we can create functions for modal instead of writing code for `ConfirmModal` everywhere, we are doing a similar thing using `ReportActionContextMenu.showDeleteModal` which eliminates code duplication.

183	Make it Easier to delete waypoints.

189	Here are the steps we should take to implement this feature:
190	
191	- Add the `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` button to `Header with back button` component [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L116), this will ensure we have the three dots menu.
192	- Add a state variable on the page [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L60) to track if the modal is open. we will name it `isModalVisible` and set the default value false.
193	  ```js
194	  const [isDeleteModalVisible, setIsDeleteModalVisible] = useState(false);
195	  ```
196	- Add the custom menu item to a const variable:
197		```js
198		const threeDotsMenuItems = {
199	        icon: Expensicons.Trashcan,
200	        translationKey: 'common.delete',
201	        action: () => setIsDeleteModalVisible(true)
202		}
203		```
204		This has the common delete text and pressing it sets the Delete Modal Visibility to `true`. We will now pass this as a prop to HeaderWithBackButton:
205		```js
206		<HeaderWithBackButton
207		      threeDotsMenuItems={threeDotsMenuItems}
208		      ...
209		/>
210		```
211	- Add the `ConfirmModal` component to the code [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L155) and make sure it is visible only while the variable we defined has the `true` value.
212		```js
213		{isDeleteModalVisible &&
214			<ConfirmModal
215	    		title={props.translate('waypoint.common.delete')}
216	    		isVisible={isDeleteModalOpen}
217	    		onConfirm={deleteWaypointAndHideModal}
218	    		onCancel={() => setIsDeleteModalVisible(false)}
219	    		prompt={props.translate('waypoint.common.deleteConfirmation')}
220	    		confirmText={props.translate('common.delete')}
221	    		cancelText={props.translate('common.cancel')}
222	    		danger
223			/>
224		}
225		```
226	
227	- Now we will be defining the `deleteWaypointAndHideModal` function which will be using the `removeWaypoint` function from [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/252cc247dbeed5b7e4b6cbaa15f91a2bdb20b520/src/libs/actions/Transaction.js#L92) (passing it the `transactionID from props` and the `waypointIndex` from the route params) and will hide the modal using `setIsDeleteModalVisible(false)`
228	
229	-  We will be adding the translations [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0b7ae7f58e3a98ba49cf9fc2812921964d9d8895/src/languages/en.js#L6) and [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0b7ae7f58e3a98ba49cf9fc2812921964d9d8895/src/languages/es.js#L5) under a `waypoint.common:` key
230

text:
Let me analyze the proposals for issue 25901. The issue is about making it easier to delete waypoints (stops) in a distance/waypoint editor.

Let me understand the problem:
- Users currently can only "delete" a waypoint by saving an empty text input, which is not discoverable
- The solution should add a three-dot menu → menu popup → delete confirmation flow

Let me evaluate each proposal:

**Proposal 0**: Suggests two approaches:
1. Three-dot menu in waypoint editor → "Remove Stop" → confirmation modal → calls `removeWaypoint`
2. Minus circle icon in the list of stops (instead of dot indicator) for direct deletion from the list
Also suggests making a common delete stop modal.

**Proposal 1**: Very brief. Suggests adding `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` to `HeaderWithBackButton` in `WaypointEditor.js`, adding trashcan icon + delete option, and adding `ConfirmModal`. Very sparse on details.

**Proposal 2**: Suggests:
1. Three-dot menu in WaypointEditor
2. Track if waypoint exists via variable
3. Register "Delete Waypoint" option in three-dot menu items
4. Use `ConfirmModal` in WaypointEditor
5. On confirm, delete waypoint data using `TransactionUtils`, then navigate back
6. Adds note about `threeDotsAnchorPosition` and `withWindowDimensions` HOC for desktop

**Proposal 3**: Suggests:
1. Pass `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` and `threeDotsMenuItems` (Delete waypoint option) to `HeaderWithBackButton`
2. Use `ConfirmModal` with proper translations (`distance.deleteStop`, `distance.deleteStopConfirmation`)
3. Delete using `Transaction.removeWaypoint` with `transactionID` and `waypointIndex` from props
4. Has a complete working video

**Proposal 4**: Very similar to 3 but:
1. Uses state variable `isDeleteModalVisible`
2. Translation key structure different - `waypoint.common.delete`, `waypoint.common.deleteConfirmation`
3. Same general approach: three-dot menu → confirm modal → call `removeWaypoint`

Let me think about what a maintainer would value:

1. **Root cause analysis**: Proposals 0-4 all correctly identify this as a feature request/new feature, not a bug. That's fine.

2. **Fix quality**: Let me compare the approaches:

- **Proposal 0** goes beyond the issue scope by suggesting two different UI approaches (three-dot menu AND a minus icon in the list). The issue specifically asks for "Dot menu icon → menu pop up → delete confirmation". Adding an extra entry point from the list is arguably scope creep. The proposal is also poorly structured.

- **Proposal 1** is too brief and lacks concrete implementation details. It doesn't address translations, proper navigation, or cleanup.

- **Proposal 2** is decent - covers the three-dot menu approach, tracks whether waypoint exists, has confirmation flow, handles navigation back. It also notes the `threeDotsAnchorPosition` and `withWindowDimensions` needed for desktop.

- **Proposal 3** is the most complete and correct:
  - Uses `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` and `threeDotsMenuItems` props on `HeaderWithBackButton`
  - Uses `ConfirmModal` with proper props and `danger` styling
  - Uses `Transaction.removeWaypoint` with correct args (`transactionID` and `waypointIndex` from props)
  - Has translation keys under `distance` namespace which is already used for distance-related translations
  - Has a complete working video demonstrating the solution
  - References specific code locations with exact prop names

- **Proposal 4** is similar to 3 but:
  - Uses `waypoint.common.delete` for translations instead of `distance.deleteStop` - less aligned with existing naming conventions
  - Wraps ConfirmModal in conditional rendering (`{isDeleteModalVisible && ...}`) which is unnecessary since ConfirmModal already has `isVisible` prop
  - Slightly less polished than 3

3. **Maintainer perspective**: The maintainer would look for:
   - Correct use of existing patterns (ThreeDotsMenu, ConfirmModal)
   - Proper translation keys
   - Following existing code conventions
   - Not over-engineering (Proposal 0 over-engineers by suggesting multiple approaches)
   - Complete, working solution (Proposal 3 has a working video)
   - Properly handling the delete action with the right Transaction method

Proposal 3 appears strongest - it:
- Stays within the scope of the issue (three-dot menu → confirmation)
- Uses correct existing patterns (`shouldShowThreeDotsButton`, `threeDotsMenuItems`, `ConfirmModal`)
- Has complete working demo
- References correct translation paths (`distance.deleteStop` which aligns with existing `distance` namespace)
- Uses `Transaction.removeWaypoint` with correct parameters
- Handles the `danger` prop for destructive action styling

Proposal 2 is also decent but less detailed about the actual implementation code.

Proposal 3 seems like the clear winner. Let me select it.

Now I need to write `manager_decisions.json` at the repo root.

Proposal 3 is the third entry (index 3), so `selected_proposal_id` should be 3.

Let me analyze each proposal as a maintainer would, evaluating root-cause analysis, fix quality, and alignment with issue requirements.

**Issue 25901** asks for: Three-dot menu icon → menu popup → delete confirmation flow for waypoints in the waypoint editor.

**Evaluating proposals:**

| # | Approach | Analysis |
|---|----------|----------|
| **0** | Two entry points (three-dot menu + minus icon in list) | ✗ Scope creep — adds a second UI entry point not requested. Over-engineers with a "common modal" abstraction. |
| **1** | Three-dot menu + ConfirmModal | ✗ Too sparse — no translations, no navigation handling, no details on the `removeWaypoint` call. |
| **2** | Three-dot menu + ConfirmModal + navigation back | ✓ Decent. Notes `threeDotsAnchorPosition` and `withWindowDimensions` for desktop. Less detailed on actual code. |
| **3** | Three-dot menu + ConfirmModal + `Transaction.removeWaypoint` | ★ **Strongest**. Complete: uses `shouldShowThreeDotsButton`/`threeDotsMenuItems`, `ConfirmModal` with `danger` prop, correct `Transaction.removeWaypoint(transactionID, waypointIndex)`, properly namespaced `distance.deleteStop` translations, working demo video. Follows existing patterns exactly. |
| **4** | Similar to 3, different translation keys | ✗ Weaker than 3: proposes `waypoint.common.delete` translations vs the more natural `distance.deleteStop`; wraps ConfirmModal in conditional rendering redundant with `isVisible` prop. |

**Winner: Proposal 3** — it follows existing component patterns precisely, uses correct props/methods, and has a verified working demo.

text: The decision has been written. Let me summarize the reasoning for the user.

Written `manager_decisions.json` with `selected_proposal_id: 3`.

**Decision rationale:** Proposal 3 is the strongest because it:

1. **Follows existing patterns** — uses `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` + `threeDotsMenuItems` on `HeaderWithBackButton` and `Transaction.removeWaypoint(transactionID, waypointIndex)`, matching the codebase conventions exactly.
2. **Correct component usage** — `ConfirmModal` with proper props (`danger` for destructive styling, correct `onConfirm`/`onCancel`, proper translations).
3. **Right translation scoping** — keys under the existing `distance` namespace (`distance.deleteStop`, `distance.deleteStopConfirmation`), aligning with the distance feature area rather than inventing a new `waypoint` namespace.
4. **Verified working** — includes a complete working demo video; the solution demonstrably works end-to-end.

The other proposals either over-scoped (0), under-specified (1), or had minor but clear quality regressions vs. proposal 3 (4's redundant conditional wrap, 2's less precise implementation detail).
